**Owning spec**: `docs/sdd/features/active/2026-06-12-kappa-cqrs-governance-root-fix/spec.md`
**Owning plan**: `docs/sdd/features/active/2026-06-12-kappa-cqrs-governance-root-fix/plan.md`

**Approved at**: 2026-06-12
**Diff**: Implementation, tests, docs, generated contracts, and SDD evidence are present in the worktree, including Asset Profile, CEX Token Profile, Token fact repository transaction guards, Queue Ops retry repository-contract guards, Token Radar serving repository transaction guards, Evidence ingest repository transaction guards, ProjectionRepository control-plane transaction guards, Token Radar rank-source repository transaction/query no-commit guards, Token Radar downstream dirty-target repository guards, AccountQualityRepository transaction guards, NotificationRepository transaction guards, NewsRepository transaction guards, and Macro projection dirty-target transaction guards; final integration-heavy completion evidence is intentionally absent because the user stopped further integration runs.

## Diff summary

Primary changed areas:

- Stocks Radar API/service/config/docs/tests remove request-time quote provider wiring and expose explicit unavailable quote state.
- Macrodata quote runtime lane deletion is now guarded by architecture tests for removed files, runtime fields, config aliases, and provider exports.
- Public API/read-model/query architecture guards now reject runtime provider wiring and integration imports on public read paths.
- Account-quality backfill writes moved out of `read_models/account_quality_service.py` into an explicit domain service; API and read-model CLI now use `AccountQualityService.from_conn(...)`, while ops remains the only backfill caller.
- Account-quality `interfaces.py` now exposes read services only; it no longer exports the SQL repository or ops backfill service as cross-domain public surface.
- Account-quality now has a domain `ARCHITECTURE.md` declaring read-model tables, ops-only maintenance writers, stable keys, and public consumers.
- Global architecture now describes Narrative Intel as current `narrative_admissions` source-set ownership plus legacy currentness reads, not as active per-mention semantics or discussion-digest LLM lanes.
- Narrative product reads no longer expose retired semantic backlog/processing state or the unused Narrative backlog health schema; selected post semantics remain read-only legacy context only.
- `resolution_refresh` manifest/runtime/tests remove old once-helper compatibility and align to the dirty lookup queue.
- `resolution_refresh` lookup running, finish, fail, and claim-completion state transitions now require `RepositorySession.transaction`; provider IO remains outside the DB transaction and runtime code no longer calls `repos.conn.commit()` directly.
- Notification repository/migration/tests add bounded stale-running cleanup and a partial stale-running index.
- Token Radar projection/tests remove retention prune work from publication and record a successor retention item.
- Token Radar architecture tests now reject retention prune calls and `pruned_*` counters in `refresh_rank_set`.
- Pulse read repository/contracts/tests remove public read JSONB event-array expansion.
- Signal Pulse public route/service/schema/generated contracts no longer expose `agent_worker_running` or read scheduler liveness.
- Token evidence, token intent, lookup-key, evidence-link, and intent-resolution fact writers now require PostgreSQL rowcount evidence before returning facts or rewrite accounting; fallback `SELECT` readback no longer proves token fact write success.
- News source status, News fetch provider-contract validation, and runtime status now read a static provider-type contract plus persisted/schema state; code no longer introspects runtime provider objects, feed-client capability methods, private registries, or Python provider-type enum fallbacks for provider contract support/schema evidence.
- News item-process agent admission now requires repository readback of the just-written item/entity/mention/fact context and fails closed instead of using worker-memory fallback context.
- News fetch page dirty targets now require repository-returned `affected_news_item_ids`; missing affected-set evidence fails the fetch run and emits no current-item fallback dirty target or wake.
- News page/brief dirty enqueue now requires `repos.news.servable_news_item_ids(...)`; missing repository filter evidence fails before enqueue instead of writing raw item ids into projection dirty targets.
- Token Radar source-event dirty work now requires `token_radar_source_dirty_events` across ingest, resolution reprocess, projection worker, and projection service; missing repository contract fails closed instead of being treated as an empty source queue.
- Token Radar source dirty event repository enqueue/claim/done/error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `token_radar_source_dirty_events` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Token Radar target dirty repository target enqueue, market enqueue, claim, bounded catch-up enqueue, done, and error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `token_radar_dirty_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Token Radar rank publication now requires callable connection transaction support before current-row publication side effects; missing or malformed support fails with `token_radar_projection_requires_transactional_connection` instead of optional probe / non-contract `TypeError` behavior.
- Token Radar dirty projection processing now wraps claimed source/target dirty work in one explicit connection transaction; source-edge writes, target-feature writes/deletes, rank publication attempts, and dirty done/error terminalization use caller-owned `commit=False` instead of autocommit fragments.
- Token Radar downstream dirty-target fan-out now requires formal repository-session dirty-target repositories for Pulse trigger, Narrative Admission, Token Profile Current, and Token Capture Tier; missing repos fail as session wiring errors instead of optional probe/custom RuntimeError branches.
- Event Anchor repository terminal transaction helper now reads the formal `conn.transaction` contract directly; missing or non-callable support fails with `event_anchor_repository_transaction_required` before job terminal or terminal-ledger SQL.
- Market Tick Current dirty repository enqueue/claim/done/error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `market_tick_current_dirty_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Token Profile Current dirty repository enqueue/claim/done/error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `token_profile_current_dirty_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Token Image Source dirty repository enqueue/claim/done/error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `token_image_source_dirty_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Asset Profile Refresh target repository enqueue/claim/reschedule/error mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `asset_profile_refresh_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Token Capture Tier dirty repository rank-set enqueue/claim/done mutations now require connection transaction support when the repository owns commit; missing support fails before `token_capture_tier_dirty_targets` SQL instead of falling back to manual `self.conn.commit()`.
- Notification worker fact/control writes now require worker-session `unit_of_work`; missing UoW support fails before writing rows instead of using `nullcontext` and manually committing a repository connection.
- Macrodata bundle import now requires `RepositorySession.unit_of_work` plus `require_transaction`; missing session support fails before writing macro observations or import runs instead of using raw `conn.transaction()` fallback.
- Pulse candidate job service now requires `RepositorySession.transaction`; missing session transaction support fails before Pulse agent ledger, eval, candidate, playbook, admission, or job terminal rows are written instead of using `nullcontext` or raw `conn.transaction()` fallback.
- News page/source-quality projection workers now require `RepositorySession.transaction`; missing session transaction support fails before dirty target claim, read-model writes, downstream page dirty enqueue, or done/error marking instead of using `nullcontext` or raw `conn.transaction()` fallback.
- Pulse candidate worker dirty-trigger claim, admission/edge/public visibility writes, job enqueue, and dirty target done/error updates now require `RepositorySession.transaction`; missing session transaction support fails before claim/write instead of using raw `conn.transaction()` fallback.
- News fetch/process/brief writer workers now require `RepositorySession.transaction`; missing session transaction support fails before source reconcile, item claim, agent admission/current brief, dirty enqueue, or failure-state writes instead of using raw `conn.transaction()` fallback.
- Macro projection dirty-target repository-owned claim/done/error mutations now require connection transaction support before `macro_projection_dirty_targets` SQL; `MacroViewProjectionWorker` keeps those writes caller-owned with `commit=False` inside `RepositorySession.transaction`.
- EventAnchorBackfill stale cleanup now requires worker-session `unit_of_work`; missing UoW support fails before stale job terminalization/reschedule or matching enriched-event lifecycle writes instead of using manual commit fallback.
- TokenCaptureTierWorker projection now requires `RepositorySession.transaction`; dirty target claim, tier row writes/demotions, and dirty target done state share that transaction instead of using claim-before-transaction or manual commit compatibility.
- EventAnchorBackfillJobRepository terminal paths now require connection transaction; missing transaction support fails before `event_anchor_backfill_jobs` terminal SQL or `worker_queue_terminal_events` terminal ledger writes instead of using `nullcontext`.
- Queue Terminal operator resolution now requires connection transaction; missing transaction support fails before `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE`, `worker_queue_terminal_events.operator_action` updates, or retry transitions instead of using `nullcontext` / manual commit compatibility.
- DiscoveryRepository terminal lookup claims now require connection transaction; missing transaction support fails before deleting `token_discovery_dirty_lookup_keys` claims or writing `worker_queue_terminal_events` ledger rows instead of using `nullcontext` / manual commit compatibility.
- NewsProjectionDirtyTargetRepository terminal paths now require connection transaction; missing transaction support fails before deleting `news_projection_dirty_targets` claims or writing `worker_queue_terminal_events` ledger rows instead of using `nullcontext` / manual commit compatibility.
- Ops projection dirty repair execute mode now requires connection transaction; missing transaction support fails before repair scans or `news_projection_dirty_targets` enqueue, while dry-run remains read-only.
- Token intent rebuild and resolution reprocess now require `RepositorySession.transaction`; direct `repos.conn.commit()` and `commit` compatibility were removed from the fact rebuild/reprocess entrypoints.
- TokenIntentResolver no longer exposes a `commit` parameter or direct `self.resolutions.conn.commit()` path; resolver persistence is caller-owned inside ingest/rebuild/reprocess transactions.
- Account Quality backfill now requires a callable connection transaction before upstream fact replay and account profile/stat/snapshot writes; naked service-owned `conn.commit()` compatibility was removed.
- Asset Market route/profile/symbol sync services now keep provider reads outside DB transactions and require a callable connection transaction for registry/profile writes; naked service-owned `conn.commit()` compatibility was removed.
- RegistryRepository repository-owned registry asset, CEX token, price-feed, US equity symbol upsert, and US equity deactivation writes now require callable connection transaction before SQL; naked `self.conn.commit()` compatibility was removed.
- DiscoveryRepository ordinary lookup queue/result enqueue, claim, done, reschedule, start, finish, and fail mutations now require callable connection transaction before SQL; naked `self.conn.commit()` compatibility was removed.
- CLI ops execute repair/sync commands now require callable connection transactions for dirty queue, News repair, and GMGN directory writes; `commit=True` and naked `.conn.commit()` compatibility were removed from `ops.py`.
- Non-integration architecture guards now cover notification bounded stale-running SQL and Pulse JSONB event-id expansion so `make check` protects those boundaries while integration gates are suspended.
- Non-integration architecture guards now keep domain read-model modules free of write/maintenance tokens and keep account-quality public read paths from importing the repository directly.
- Chinese review note `docs/reviews/kappa-cqrs-root-cause-analysis-zh-2026-06-12.md` records root-cause analysis, architecture comparison, SQL posture, and verification boundaries.
- Generated DB schema/OpenAPI/types/SDD work index were regenerated where drift was detected.

Migrations added:

- `src/parallax/platform/db/alembic/versions/20260612_0178_notification_delivery_stale_claim_index.py`

Schema or contract changes that consumers must be aware of:

- `/api/stocks-radar` quote blocks now report explicit unavailable read-model state instead of request-time provider snapshots.
- Pulse handle filtering is direct subject-key based and no longer expands candidate event-id JSONB arrays.
- `/api/signal-lab/pulse` health no longer exposes `agent_worker_running`; worker liveness remains available through status/ops diagnostics.
- `/api/news/sources/status`, `news_fetch`, and `/api/status.news_provider_contract` report supported provider types from `RUNTIME_SUPPORTED_NEWS_PROVIDER_TYPES`, not from the runtime provider object. `news_fetch` reads schema provider types from `news_source_provider_constraint_values()` instead of `PROVIDER_TYPES`.
- `news_item_process` requires complete repository admission context after deterministic writes; missing context is retried/terminalized instead of being filled from worker memory.
- `news_fetch` requires canonical repository `affected_news_item_ids` for inserted/updated items; missing affected-set evidence fails the fetch run instead of enqueueing a fallback page dirty target.
- News projection dirty enqueue requires the repository `servable_news_item_ids` filter for page/brief targets; missing filter evidence fails closed.
- Token Radar source-edge dirty enqueue/claim paths require the `token_radar_source_dirty_events` repository contract; test fakes and runtime wiring must model that queue explicitly.
- TokenRadarSourceDirtyEventRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before source dirty event enqueue/claim/done/error SQL.
- TokenRadarDirtyTargetRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before target dirty enqueue/market enqueue/claim/catch-up enqueue/done/error SQL.
- TokenRadarProjection direct callers and test fakes must provide `repos.conn.transaction`; missing or malformed transaction support now fails before dirty queue claim/processing SQL.
- TokenRadarProjection downstream dirty-target fan-out requires `pulse_trigger_dirty_targets`, `narrative_admission_dirty_targets`, `token_profile_current_dirty_targets`, and `token_capture_tier_dirty_targets` on the repository session; missing attributes now fail as wiring errors.
- PulseEvidenceBuilder source repositories must expose `list_source_events`, `list_enriched_events`, `list_market_facts`, `list_identity_facts`, and `get_current_discussion_digest`; missing methods now fail as wiring errors before a sealed evidence packet is built.
- `notification_rule` worker sessions must expose `unit_of_work`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before notification facts or delivery control rows are written.
- NotificationRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit for notification fact insertion/aggregation, read-marker writes, delivery enqueue, or failed/dead delivery requeue; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before `notifications`, `notification_reads`, or `notification_deliveries` SQL.
- NewsRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit for source/fetch/provider item/canonical item/deterministic fact/agent run/brief/source-quality/page-row writes; worker/session paths pass `commit=False` inside `RepositorySession.transaction`, while direct callers that omit `transaction` now fail before News SQL.
- MacroIntelRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit for macro projection dirty-target claim/done/error writes; worker/session paths pass `commit=False` inside `RepositorySession.transaction`, while direct callers that omit `transaction` now fail before `macro_projection_dirty_targets` SQL.
- Macrodata bundle import sessions must expose `unit_of_work` and `require_transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit them now fail before macro facts, import audit rows, or projection dirty targets are written.
- Pulse candidate job service sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before Pulse agent run/step/eval/candidate/playbook/admission/job terminal rows are written.
- News projection worker sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before `news_page_rows`, `news_source_quality_rows`, `news_sources.source_quality_status`, page dirty enqueue, or dirty-target terminal state writes.
- Pulse candidate worker sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before dirty-target claim, admission/edge/public visibility writes, job enqueue, or dirty-target done/error writes.
- News fetch/process/brief writer worker sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before News source reconcile, item claim, deterministic fact writes, agent admission/current brief writes, projection dirty enqueue, or failure-state writes.
- EventAnchorBackfillWorker sessions must expose `unit_of_work` for stale cleanup; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before event-anchor job terminal writes or enriched-event lifecycle terminal writes.
- TokenCaptureTierWorker sessions must expose `transaction` and `require_transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit them now fail before dirty target claim, tier row writes/demotions, or dirty target done writes.
- EventAnchorBackfillJobRepository connections must expose `transaction`; tests and direct repository callers that omit it now fail before event-anchor job terminal writes or terminal-ledger writes.
- Queue Terminal operator resolution connections must expose `transaction`; tests and direct platform callers that omit it now fail before terminal-event row locks or operator action writes.
- DiscoveryRepository connections must expose `transaction`; tests and direct repository callers that omit it now fail before terminal lookup claim delete or terminal-ledger writes.
- ResolutionRefreshWorker sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before lookup running/finish/fail/claim-completion writes, while provider IO remains outside the DB transaction.
- NotificationDeliveryWorker sessions must expose `transaction`; tests and runtime sessions that omit it now fail before delivery claim/pre-flight transition writes, while Apprise/PushDeer IO remains outside the DB transaction.
- Token intent rebuild/reprocess sessions must expose `transaction` and `require_transaction`; tests and runtime callers that omit them now fail before token evidence/intent/lookup/resolution/discovery/source-dirty writes.
- TokenIntentResolver callers no longer pass `commit`; test fakes that asserted the compatibility argument must instead model caller-owned persistence.
- AccountQualityBackfillService repository connections must expose `transaction`; test fakes and ops callers that omit it now fail before upstream fact replay or account profile/stat/snapshot writes.
- Asset Market sync service registry/profile connections must expose `transaction`; test fakes and ops callers that omit it now fail before CEX route/profile or US equity symbol writes.
- RegistryRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before registry asset, CEX route, price-feed, or US equity symbol SQL.
- DiscoveryRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns ordinary lookup queue/result commits; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before lookup-key enqueue, due-claim, done, reschedule, start, finish, or fail SQL.
- CLI ops execute repository connections must expose `transaction`; dry-run remains read-only, and GMGN directory provider iteration is completed before DB write transactions open.
- NewsProjectionDirtyTargetRepository connections must expose `transaction`; tests and direct repository callers that omit it now fail before projection dirty-target claim delete or terminal-ledger writes.
- Ops projection dirty repair connections must expose `transaction` for execute mode; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before repair scans or dirty-target enqueue.
- AssetProfileRefreshTargetRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before provider-profile refresh enqueue/claim/reschedule/error SQL.
- TokenCaptureTierDirtyTargetRepository connections must expose `transaction` when the repository owns commit; tests and direct callers that omit it now fail before capture-tier rank-set enqueue/claim/done SQL.
- Asset Market provider wiring treats a configured GMGN DEX provider object as a concrete protocol contract: test fakes and runtime adapters must expose `token_quotes(...)` and `token_profile(...)`; malformed objects now fail wiring and partial cleanup instead of silently routing quote reads to OKX or dropping the GMGN profile source.
- CEX Market Intel CoinGlass provider wiring reads `settings.workers.cex_oi_radar_board.enabled` and `coinglass_enrichment_limit` directly; malformed worker settings now fail as runtime configuration errors instead of being interpreted as enabled/default-zero enrichment state.
- Asset Market startup failure cleanup reads `OkxProviderBundle` fields directly once the bundle object exists; malformed OKX bundle fields are recorded as cleanup failure notes on the original startup error while any readable provider handles are still closed.
- Pulse decision provider timeout wiring reads `workers.agent_runtime.lanes["pulse.decision"].timeout_seconds` directly; malformed agent-runtime settings now fail instead of using a provider-local 120-second timeout fallback.
- `MarketTickPollWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.market_tick_poll`, the Asset Market provider bundle, and DB pool bundle; legacy constructor provider args, `batch_size`/`interval_seconds` overrides, and settings/provider `SimpleNamespace` synthesis are removed.
- CLI ops one-shot worker setting overrides require formal Pydantic `model_copy(update=...)`; `model_dump`, `vars(...)`, `__dict__`, and `SimpleNamespace(**...)` settings synthesis are removed from production ops code.
- `WorkerBase` reads `settings.enabled`, `settings.interval_seconds`, and `settings.backoff.base_ms/max_ms` directly; malformed core worker settings now fail instead of becoming enabled workers with base-class fallback cadence/backoff.
- `TokenCaptureTierWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.token_capture_tier` and the DB pool bundle; legacy `db`, `batch_size`, `ws_limit`, `poll_limit`, and `interval_seconds` constructor compatibility paths are removed. The worker reads `batch_size`, `ws_limit`, `poll_limit`, and `lease_ms` directly, and `project_once(...)` now requires explicit projection limits from its caller.
- `EventAnchorBackfillWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.event_anchor_backfill`, the DB pool bundle, and the Asset Market provider bundle unless an explicit capture service is injected; legacy `db`, `wake_bus`, individual `dex_quote_market` / `cex_market` handles, constructor limit/window overrides, local `DEFAULT_*` values, and settings `SimpleNamespace` synthesis are removed. The worker reads event-anchor batch, concurrency, retry, lease, age/window, lag, and statement-timeout settings directly.
- `TokenProfileCurrentWorker` reads `statement_timeout_seconds`, `batch_size`, `lease_ms`, and `retry_ms` directly from formal `settings.workers.token_profile_current`; `retry_ms` is now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, worker-local `DEFAULT_LEASE_MS` / `DEFAULT_RETRY_MS` are removed, and `rebuild_token_profile_current_once(...)` requires explicit limit/lease/retry arguments from its caller.
- `MarketTickCurrentProjectionWorker` reads `statement_timeout_seconds`, `batch_size`, `lease_ms`, and `retry_ms` directly from formal `settings.workers.market_tick_current_projection`; worker-local retry defaults, hard-coded batch/lease fallbacks, and settings `getattr(..., default)` probes are removed.
- `TokenImageMirrorWorker` reads `statement_timeout_seconds`, `batch_size`, `lease_ms`, and `retry_ms` directly from formal `settings.workers.token_image_mirror`; `retry_ms` is now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, and worker-local lease/retry defaults plus hard-coded statement-timeout/batch fallbacks are removed.
- `AssetProfileRefreshWorker` reads `statement_timeout_seconds`, `batch_size`, `lease_ms`, and `provider_retry_ms` directly from formal `settings.workers.asset_profile_refresh`; `provider_retry_ms` is now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, and worker-local lease/provider-retry defaults plus hard-coded batch fallbacks are removed.
- `MarketTickStreamWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.market_tick_stream`, the DB pool bundle, and the configured stream provider; `stream_cycle_seconds` is now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, the factory no longer passes duplicate subscription limits, and worker-local `SimpleNamespace` settings synthesis, `db` / `wake_bus` aliases, constructor interval/limit/cycle overrides, and local stream default constants are removed.
- `ResolutionRefreshWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.resolution_refresh` and the configured discovery provider; constructor chain overrides, unused quote-provider injection, `wake_bus` aliases, discovery/reprocess limit fallbacks, and settings `getattr(..., default)` probes are removed.
- `LivePriceGateway` construction requires formal `settings.workers.live_price_gateway` and the DB pool bundle; `target_limit` and `target_ttl_seconds` are now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, the factory no longer passes provider bundles or duplicate interval overrides, and worker-local `SimpleNamespace` settings synthesis plus `DEFAULT_LIVE_TARGET_*` constants are removed.
- `NewsPageProjectionWorker` reads `statement_timeout_seconds`, `batch_size`, `lease_ms`, and `retry_ms` directly from formal `settings.workers.news_page_projection`; those fields are now in the formal worker settings schema/default workers YAML, and runtime settings fallback probes plus hard-coded claim/retry defaults are removed.
- `MacroViewProjectionWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.macro_view_projection` and the DB pool bundle; `lease_ms` and `retry_ms` are explicit formal worker settings, history lower bounds live in the settings schema, the factory injects `wake_emitter`, claim limit now reads `batch_size`, and runtime history/lease/retry fallback constants plus old `wake_bus`/`**kwargs` constructor paths are removed.
- `NewsSourceQualityProjectionWorker` construction requires formal `settings.workers.news_source_quality_projection` and the DB pool bundle; `lease_ms`, `retry_ms`, and `statement_timeout_seconds` are explicit formal worker settings, the factory injects `wake_emitter`, and runtime statement-timeout/batch/lease/retry/window fallback probes plus old `wake_bus`/`**kwargs` constructor paths are removed.

## Risks observed

- Final full repository verification remains unproven because integration-heavy gates were stopped by user direction.
- The integration suite is slow because API/macro scenarios repeatedly invoke `macrodata bundle history`; this is a test/runtime performance risk separate from the Kappa/CQRS fixes.

## Follow-ups

- A persisted US equity quote read model should be specified in a separate feature if product needs live quote data in `/api/stocks-radar`.
